response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems developer with a long track record, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The single hardest requirement — native-level Japanese language proficiency — is entirely absent from his profile; he lists only English, Romanian, and basic German. Beyond that, his experience is concentrated in web, mobile, and cloud-native stacks (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, React), with only surface-level C/C++ exposure from a short NI stint and a university thesis. He has no demonstrable experience in embedded firmware, LAN-switch products, Linux kernel modification, or any of the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC auth).
strengths: Extensive overall software engineering career (17+ years) with demonstrable ability to work across multiple languages and paradigms
Some low-level systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments and a wave-field synthesis thesis using Linux and C++
Familiarity with Linux and REST APIs, which are peripheral to the preferred skills list
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement explicitly stated in the job description
No embedded networking or firmware experience; no exposure to LAN-switch architecture, L2/L3 networking, or chip-vendor SDKs
No security domain knowledge: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, and attack/tamper detection are completely absent from his history

key_factors.factor: Weak C and Linux kernel background
